question.label: Historical tradeoffs
question.name: Which tradeoffs feel hardest to place without the backstory?
question.description: Drills into the specific missing historical context he named so the manager can target the gap.

answer: I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.

assessment.note: He names a specific clarity gap: unclear domain ownership when priorities conflict.

new_queue.label: Domain ownership
new_queue.name: When priorities conflict, where does domain ownership get murky for you?

new_queue.label: Context moments
new_queue.name: When do you find yourself needing more context before you move?

new_queue.label: Architecture assumptions
new_queue.name: What kinds of architecture calls feel easiest to misread here?

new_queue.label: Useful introductions
new_queue.name: Who would be most useful for you to spend live time with next?

new_queue.label: Steady next steps
new_queue.name: What should we line up before our next check-in?

history.answer_excerpt: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
